frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Dekho, job important hai, but bachche honge toh priority thodi adjust karni padti hai na. Father kitna bhi involved ho, maa jaisa dhyaan kaun rakhega? Career toh baad mein bhi grow ho sakta hai, bachpan wapas nahi aata.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare as primarily the mother's responsibility and frames a woman's career as secondary once children are involved. It encodes the surface manifestations of career sacrifice framing and the belief that maternal care is uniquely necessary while fathers are implicitly less responsible.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
